title: crotalaria phyllostachya
intro: Crotalaria phyllostachya, also known as the golden bush or stickpea, is a plant species within the legume family that is native to tropical Africa and Asia. The plant contains medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The seeds of Crotalaria phyllostachya have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal purposes.
